note: Submodule of email_registration. Makes the full email the username, keeps both in sync, and can override the displayed name. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts (the username strategy, the sync rule, the display-override setting/modes, the permission that bypasses it, the swapped bulk action) an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: How does the Email Registration Username submodule differ from the main Email Registration module in what it stores as the username?
evals.expected_output: It stores the full email address as the username (via hook_email_registration_name_alter setting $newAccountName to $account->getEmail()), whereas the main module stores only the part before the @.

evals.prompt: With Email Registration Username enabled, if an admin changes an existing user's email address, does the username change too? Under what condition?
evals.expected_output: It re-syncs the username to the new email only if the old username was already equal to the old email; a user who chose a different custom username keeps it.

evals.prompt: Which config key and modes let Email Registration Username hide the email from other users' view of the display name?
evals.expected_output: username_display_override_mode on email_registration_username.settings, with modes disabled / email_registration (part before @) / custom (username_display_custom, supports tokens).

evals.prompt: In the Email Registration Username submodule, which permission causes the display-name override to be skipped so the viewer sees the real name/email?
evals.expected_output: The core 'view user email addresses' permission — hook_user_format_name_alter returns early (no override) for a current user who has it.

evals.prompt: I want to display '****' or a token-based value instead of users' email addresses with Email Registration Username. Which mode and config key do I use, and does it support tokens?
evals.expected_output: Set username_display_override_mode to 'custom' and put the text or token in username_display_custom (e.g. '****' or [user:...]); it is passed through the token service, so tokens are supported (the module depends on token).

evals.prompt: With the Email Registration Username submodule enabled, what does the 'Update username (from email_registration)' bulk action do differently?
evals.expected_output: It swaps the action class (via hook_action_info_alter) to UpdateUsernameWithMailAction, which sets each selected user's username to their full email address rather than a stripped/generated name.
